title: Active Agent Claims Registry
description: Live, signal-like registry of which agents are currently working on which areas of the repo. Advisory, not enforcing. Claims inform agent judgement; the protocol does not refuse entry to claimed areas. Governed by .agent/directives/agent-collaboration.md.
$comment_compatibility: Additive-only schema evolution within a major version. v1.x agents reading v1.y files (y > x) ignore unrecognised fields and preserve them on write-back; v1.2.0 also treats area.kind enum expansion as additive, so older runtime readers preserve unknown area kinds as opaque claimed areas rather than rejecting the registry. major-version mismatch causes the agent to bail out with an error pointing at the protocol upgrade. additionalProperties is set to false on the authored shape for the matching schema_version: it constrains agents who are AUTHORING entries against this schema (use only the named fields; new fields require a coordinated schema_version bump), and constrains the consolidate-docs § 7e schema-validation step. The maintained latest schema validates supported v1.x files and narrows v1.1.0 validation where enum shape changed; do not validate v1.2.0 files with an older v1.1.0 schema. It is NOT a runtime read-gate at session-open: agents reading entries written under a newer-minor schema preserve unrecognised fields and opaque enum values rather than rejecting the file. The runtime contract is preservation; the authoring/validation contract is no-free-form-expansion. Claim closure metadata is defined here so closed-claims.schema.json can reuse the claim contract without duplicating agent identity or area shapes; closure remains a semantic anomaly in active-claims.json and is required only by closed-claims.schema.json.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this registry schema. Version 1.1.0 adds closure metadata definitions for closed-claims reuse while keeping active entries advisory. Version 1.2.0 adds the git area kind for short-lived commit-window claims. Version 1.3.0 adds the ordered advisory commit_queue for staged-bundle ownership.

commit_queue.$comment_provenance: Observed + owner-directed. PDR-029 Class A.3 queue artefact for staged-bundle ownership.
commit_queue.description: Ordered advisory FIFO queue for commit-window ownership. Array order is the queue order; this is not a mechanical lock. Successful commits remove their queue entry.

claims.description: Currently-active claims. Empty array means no agent has registered an active claim. Stale claims are archived to closed-claims.archive.json by consolidate-docs.

claim_id.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Registry entries need identity; the originating shared-communication-log shape was append-only and did not carry a stable claim id.
claim_id.description: Unique identifier for this claim entry. UUID v4 recommended; any string that is unique across the active and archived registries is acceptable.

thread.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Cross-thread visibility requires an explicit slug; the originating shared-communication-log relied on prose context.
thread.description: Slug of the thread this claim is on (e.g. 'agentic-engineering-enhancements'). Matches a thread record at .agent/memory/operational/threads/<slug>.next-session.md.

areas.$comment_provenance: Observed. Shared-communication-log entries already used touched-area path patterns; v1.2.0 added the 'git' kind for short-lived commit-window claims as a first-principles addition.
areas.description: Areas of the repo this claim covers. At least one area required.

claimed_at.$comment_provenance: Observed. Every shared-communication-log entry timestamped UTC ISO 8601 with trailing Z.
claimed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this claim was registered.

freshness_seconds.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Liveness signal for stale audit; the default rationale is in collaboration-state-conventions.md §Default freshness_seconds = 14400.
freshness_seconds.description: How long this claim should be treated as current. After claimed_at + freshness_seconds, the claim is stale (noise to be audited at consolidation, not a blocker that strands other agents). Default 14400 (4 hours).

heartbeat_at.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Long-session freshness refresh; supports the heartbeat recipe in collaboration-state-lifecycle.md §Refresh During Work.
heartbeat_at.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp updated by the agent during long sessions to keep a claim fresh. Absence means the claim is as fresh as claimed_at.

sidebar_open.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Liveness hint pointing at conversation/sidebar state; the conversation file owns sidebar lifecycle.
sidebar_open.description: Whether a sidebar conversation is currently open against this claim. The conversation file owns sidebar lifecycle; this field is only a liveness hint.

intent.$comment_provenance: Observed. Every shared-communication-log entry carried an action / intent line; intent prose still carries strength under the single-level claim model.
intent.description: Short free-form description of what the agent intends. Strength of signal is communicated through the prose here ('atomic refactor in progress, please coordinate' vs 'routine edits, ping me if you also touch this'). Single-level claim model — no exclusivity gradient field; intent prose carries strength.

notes.$comment_provenance: Observed. Shared-communication-log entries carried a coordination-note paragraph alongside the intent.
notes.description: Optional free-form coordination note for whoever reads this entry.

intent_to_commit.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Convenience pointer; the root commit_queue remains authoritative for queue ordering and lifecycle.
intent_to_commit.description: Optional pointer to this claim's current entry in the root commit_queue. The queue owns ordering and lifecycle.

archived_at.description: Optional ISO 8601 date set by consolidate-docs § 7e when archiving a stale claim to closed-claims.archive.json. Presence indicates the entry has been archived; absence indicates it is (or was) active. This field should only appear in closed-claims.archive.json — its presence in active-claims.json is a semantic anomaly that consolidate-docs surfaces for owner review.

closure.description: Structured closure metadata required in closed-claims.archive.json. Presence in active-claims.json is a semantic anomaly that consolidate-docs surfaces for owner review.

handoff_record_path.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Additive optional pointer per ADR-182 + PDR-063; existing readers ignore the field without breakage.
handoff_record_path.description: Optional. Presence signals this claim is mid-cycle and carries a handoff record per PDR-063 + ADR-182; absence signals normal active-claim semantics. Value is a repo-root-relative path to a file under .agent/state/collaboration/handoffs/ (content-addressed by claim_id, with versioned successors <claim_id>.<n>.json). Readers that do not understand this field MUST ignore it (additive-extension discipline per PDR-049 + PDR-050).

intent_id.description: Unique identifier for this queued commit intent.

claim_id.description: Active claim this queue entry belongs to.

files.description: Repository-relative file paths the agent intends to stage.

commit_subject.description: Draft commit subject so peers can see the intended durable history label before it lands.

updated_at.description: Refreshed by the owning agent on commit-queue phase changes.

expires_at.description: Wall-clock 'expired' timestamp per the four-term vocabulary in collaboration-state-conventions.md. Expiry does not auto-remove or auto-resolve the queue entry.

phase.description: Advisory lifecycle phase. Successful commits remove their queue entry rather than preserving phase=complete.

staged_bundle_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint over git diff --cached --name-status and git diff --cached --full-index --binary.

staged_name_status.description: Recorded git diff --cached --name-status output for staged-bundle inspection.

notes.description: Optional free-form coordination note for this queued commit intent.

agent_id.$comment_provenance: Originally observed: every shared-communication-log entry carried (agent_name, platform, model, session_id_prefix). Amended 2026-05-26 per PDR-076a §Cascade item 1 to add optional UUID v5 id as the canonical routing disambiguator; session_id_prefix retained for readable diagnostic prose.
agent_id.description: Per PDR-027 agent identity schema as amended by PDR-076a. Identity routing key is (agent_name, id); session_id_prefix remains for readable diagnostic prose.

agent_name.description: Persistent descriptive name for this agent on this thread.

platform.description: Platform identifier: claude-code, cursor, codex, gemini, etc.

session_id_prefix.description: First 6 characters of the harness session ID; 'unknown' if not exposed.

id.description: PDR-076a §Cascade item 3 deterministic UUID v5 derived from the stable session seed. Optional during the additive migration window; promoted to required when the legacy-fallback diagnostic count reaches zero across one calendar week (PDR-076a §Cascade item 2 sunset).

kind.description: Kind of area being claimed. 'files' for path globs; 'workspace' for a pnpm workspace name; 'plan' for a plan file slug; 'adr' for an ADR number; 'git' for shared git transaction surfaces such as index/head.

patterns.description: Patterns identifying the area. Globs for files; workspace names for workspace; plan file paths for plan; ADR numbers (e.g. 'ADR-163') for adr; git transaction labels (e.g. 'index/head') for git.

kind.$comment_provenance: Observed + first-principles. Explicit closes were observed in shared-comms-log entries; stale and owner-forced close types were added from first principles to make janitor and owner-directed lifecycle paths visible.
kind.description: How the claim left the active registry: normal agent close, staleness archival, or owner-directed close.

closed_at.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Durable closure provenance; the originating shared-communication-log relied on prose timestamps.
closed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the claim was closed or archived.

closed_by.$comment_provenance: First-principles. Durable closure provenance; reuses the PDR-027 identity block.

evidence.$comment_provenance: Observed. Claim outcomes need durable references to logs, claims, plans, napkin, and threads; the evidence-ref enum was distilled from real shared-comms-log practice.
evidence.description: Structured references that justify or explain the closure.

kind.description: Kind of repository evidence the reference points at.

ref.description: Relative repository path, claim_id, commit hash, or durable evidence anchor.

summary.description: Why this evidence supports the closure.
